steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Count the relevant faces, dimensions, or scale factor before setting up equations. Lengths scale by $k$, areas by $k^2$, and volumes by $k^3$; for boxes and prisms, write the needed face or volume formula directly.

steps.title: Find the area of circle K using its radius
steps.content: Circle $K$ has radius $r = 6$ mm. Using the circle area formula, we get $$A_K = \pi(6)^2 = 36\pi \text{ mm}^2$$

steps.title: Add the areas of circles K and L
steps.content: We have the area of circle $K$ as $36\pi$ mm$^2$ and the area of circle $L$ as $121\pi$ mm$^2$ (given). The total area is $$A_{\text{total}} = 36\pi + 121\pi = 157\pi \text{ mm}^2$$ That confirms D is correct.
